rationale: For a driver app used in a van under direct Queensland sunlight, extreme legibility trumps all. We use a Swiss brutalist approach: stark white backgrounds, jet-black typography, and high-visibility Safety Orange for primary actions. Everything is built with huge tap targets (>=56px) for glove-wearing drivers.

mono.usage: Bin Labels (A1, B2), parcel counts, step indicators. Monospace ensures uniform character width for rapid scanning while driving.

spacing_and_layout.tap_targets: MINIMUM 56px height (h-14) and width (w-14) for all interactive elements (buttons, checkboxes, grid cells).
spacing_and_layout.gap: gap-4 to gap-6 for lists, gap-2 for tight groupings.
spacing_and_layout.border_radius: rounded-none or rounded-md (max 8px). No pill-shaped buttons to maximize hit area.

buttons.primary: h-14 bg-primary text-white font-bold text-lg rounded-md active:scale-95 transition-transform shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]
buttons.secondary: h-14 bg-surface text-text_primary font-bold text-lg rounded-md border-2 border-text_primary active:bg-surface_active shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]
buttons.icon_only: h-14 w-14 flex items-center justify-center bg-surface border-2 border-text_primary rounded-md active:bg-surface_active shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]

bin_grid.container: grid gap-3 p-3 bg-surface rounded-md border-2 border-text_primary
bin_grid.cell: h-20 sm:h-24 flex flex-col items-center justify-center border-4 border-text_primary bg-white rounded-md text-3xl font-mono font-black active:bg-primary active:text-white active:border-primary transition-colors shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]

photo_capture.button: h-24 w-full bg-text_primary text-white font-bold text-xl flex flex-col items-center justify-center rounded-md active:scale-[0.98] shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]
photo_capture.preview: h-48 w-full object-cover rounded-md border-4 border-text_primary shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]

multi_parcel_indicator.container: inline-flex items-center gap-2 bg-text_primary text-white px-3 py-2 rounded-md font-mono text-sm sm:text-base font-bold

configure_van_grid.layout: Vertical stack. Top: Title and description. Middle: 3 large toggle buttons (2x3, 3x3, 3x4). Below: Interactive visual preview of the grid using CSS Grid. Bottom: Sticky footer with 'Print/Export PDF' (secondary) and 'Save Configuration' (primary) buttons.

load_van.purpose: Loading stops in reverse optimized order with photo capture.
load_van.layout: Header with Progress Bar ('7 of 23 loaded') and 'Reverse Optimised Order' banner. Scrollable list of Stop Cards. The 'Next' button is fixed at the bottom once all stops are loaded.

stop_card.layout: Thick bordered brutalist card. Top row: Stop number (e.g., #23) and Address (h3). Middle row: Huge 'Suggested Bin' block (A1) on left, huge 'Camera' button on right. If photo is taken, show thumbnail. Bottom row: 'Accept' full-width button (if not accepted), or 'Override Bin' secondary button.

stop_detail.purpose: Driver is driving/arrived. Needs instant bin info.
stop_detail.layout: Bottom-drawer or full-screen card overlaying the map. Left side: Address and Multi-parcel indicator (e.g., '3 parcels: A1, A1, B2'). Right side: GIANT Bin Label (text-[6rem] sm:text-[8rem] font-mono font-black) in a bright orange box. Below: Massive photo thumbnail that expands on tap. Bottom: 'Moved it?' (Re-bin) small secondary button.
stop_detail.visual_hierarchy: 1. BIN LABEL (Giant, unmissable). 2. Package Photo. 3. Address. 4. Re-bin action.

re_bin_modal.layout: Full-screen modal or bottom sheet. Shows the Van Grid UI (same as setup). Driver taps a new cell (e.g., C3). Modal closes instantly on tap.

accessibility_and_environment.sun_glare: Absolute black (#09090B) text on absolute white (#FFFFFF) backgrounds. High contrast borders (4px thick) to separate elements without relying on subtle drop shadows.
accessibility_and_environment.glove_operation: No tap targets smaller than 56x56px. Minimum 16px gaps between interactive elements to prevent mis-taps in a moving van.
accessibility_and_environment.one_handed: Critical actions (camera, confirm) placed in the lower 50% of the screen. Top-right is reserved for passive display (Bin Label).
accessibility_and_environment.cognitive_load: Monospace fonts for alphanumeric codes (A1, B2) to prevent misreading '8' as 'B' or '1' as 'I'. No complex menus. Every action is exposed as a chunky button.
accessibility_and_environment.testing: All interactive elements must include 'data-testid' attributes (e.g., data-testid='camera-button').

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Use React JS with Tailwind CSS to build these prototypes. 2. Implement the 'Swiss Brutalist' styling exactly: thick borders (border-4 border-text_primary), hard shadows (shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B]), and strictly black/white/orange colors. 3. Ensure all buttons use 'h-14' minimum. 4. Use 'font-mono' for all Bin Labels (e.g., A1, B2). 5. Make the Bin Label on the Stop Detail screen obscenely large (e.g., text-[6rem] or 8rem). 6. Use the provided image URLs as placeholders for package photos. 7. Focus on extreme legibility for outdoor use. Do NOT use soft glassmorphism or subtle gradients.
